name: New Year's
description: Auld Lang Syne, Times Square ball drop, resolutions, and global midnight traditions.

questions.q: What song is traditionally sung at midnight on New Year's Eve in English-speaking countries?
choices: What Are You Doing New Year's Eve

source.notes: Scottish poem by Robert Burns (1788). Title roughly means 'old long ago.'

questions.q: Which Scottish poet wrote the lyrics to 'Auld Lang Syne'?
Where does the famous New Year's Eve ball drop happen in the U.S.?
Which Pacific country is generally first to celebrate the New Year?

source.notes: Kiribati's Line Islands actually celebrate first (UTC+14), but New Zealand is the first major country.

questions.q: What was the early Roman calendar's New Year's Day before Julius Caesar's reform?

source.notes: Caesar moved it to January 1 in 45 BC.

questions.q: Which technology fear gripped the world at midnight Dec 31, 1999 → Jan 1, 2000?

source.notes: Concerned 2-digit year code rolling over. Few major incidents occurred.

questions.q: Which industry sees a huge spike in memberships every January from resolutions?
Which Spanish New Year's tradition has people eating 12 grapes at midnight (one per stroke)?
choices: Las doce uvas de la suerte

source.notes: 'The 12 Lucky Grapes' — for good luck each month.

questions.q: Which Australian harbor city famously launches major fireworks first at New Year?

source.notes: Sydney Harbour Bridge fireworks; visible globally on TV.

questions.q: Which Scottish New Year's celebration features torchlight processions and 'first-footing'?
What lunar new year do many Asian countries observe in late January or February?

source.notes: Tet is the Vietnamese name for the same holiday.

questions.q: How many years' cycle is the Chinese zodiac (1 animal per year)?
What lucky food do many Americans eat on New Year's Day in the South?

source.notes: Often paired with collard greens (for money) and cornbread (for gold).

questions.q: Who hosted the long-running 'Dick Clark's New Year's Rockin' Eve' starting in 1972?

source.notes: Ryan Seacrest took over after Clark's 2012 death.

questions.q: Which Persian New Year falls on the spring equinox (March 20-21)?
Which Jewish New Year falls in September or October?

source.notes: Marks the Jewish high holy days.

questions.q: Which Brazilian New Year tradition involves dressing in white at the beach?

source.notes: Wearing white symbolizes peace; Copacabana Beach is the most famous celebration.

questions.q: What's the name of the parade held annually in Pasadena on January 1?

source.notes: Since 1890. Followed by the Rose Bowl football game.

questions.q: Which Venetian-style firework tradition does Italy celebrate on New Year's Eve?

source.notes: Italians also wear red underwear for luck.

questions.q: What's the cocktail traditionally served at midnight on New Year's Eve?
